volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section prologue.pr.1 - Section 1.1.80)

sections.label: Section prologue.pr.1
sections.translation: ’ Tis better for your loins to be stretched [*] , and for you to arise . A long play of Plautus is coming upon the stage .

sections.label: Section 1.1.3
sections.original: Si ex te tacente fieri possem certior ,
sections.translation: If , master , by your being silent , I could be informed what miseries are afflicting you so sadly ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.5
sections.original: duorúm labori ego hominum parsissem lubens ,
sections.translation: I would willingly have spared the trouble of two persons — of myself in asking you , and of yourself in answering me . Since , however , that cannot be , necessity compels me to enquire of you . Answer me : What ’ s the reason that , out of spirits for these many days past ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.10
sections.original: gestas tabellas tecum , eas lacrumis lavis ,
sections.translation: you ’ ve been carrying a letter about with you , washing it with your tears , and making no person the sharer of your purpose ? Speak out , that what I am ignorant of , I may know together with yourself .

sections.label: Section 1.1.13
sections.translation: I am wretchedly miserable , Pseudolus .

sections.label: Section 1.1.13b
sections.translation: May Jupiter forbid it !

sections.label: Section 1.1.14b
sections.original: Nihil hoc Iovis ad iudicium attinet :
sections.translation: This belongs not at all to the arbitration of Jupiter ;

sections.label: Section 1.1.15
sections.original: sub Veneris regno vapulo , non sub Iovis .
sections.translation: under the sway of Venus [*] am I harassed , not under that of Jove .

sections.label: Section 1.1.16
sections.original: Licet me id scire quid sit ? nam tu me antidhac
sections.translation: Is it allowable for me to know what it is ? For hitherto you have had me as chief confidant in your plans .

sections.label: Section 1.1.18
sections.translation: The same is now my intention .

sections.label: Section 1.1.18b
sections.translation: Let me know then what ’ s the matter with you . I ’ ll aid you either with resources , or with my efforts , or with good counsel .

sections.label: Section 1.1.20
sections.original: Cape has tabellas , tute hinc narrato tibi
sections.translation: Do you take this letter : do you thence inform yourself what misery and what care are wasting me away .

sections.label: Section 1.1.22
sections.original: Mos tibi geretur . sed quid hoc , quaeso ?
sections.translation: Compliance shall be given you . But , prithee , how ’ s this ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.22b
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.23
sections.original: Vt opinor , quaerunt litterae hae sibi liberos :
sections.translation: As I think , these letters are very loving ; they are climbing on each other ’ s backs .

sections.label: Section 1.1.24b
sections.translation: Are you making sport of me with your foolery ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.25
sections.original: Has quidem pol credo nisi Sibulla legerit ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I really do believe that unless the Sibyl [*] can read them , nobody else can possibly interpret them .

sections.label: Section 1.1.27
sections.translation: Why speak you unkindly of those sweet letters — sweet tablets too , written upon by a hand as sweet .

sections.label: Section 1.1.29
sections.original: An , opsecro hercle , habent quas gallinae manus ?
sections.translation: Troth now , have hens , prithee , such hands ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.30
sections.translation: For certainly a hen has written these letters .

sections.label: Section 1.1.30b
sections.translation: You are annoying me . Either read it or return the letter .

sections.label: Section 1.1.31b
sections.translation: Very well then , I ’ ll read it through . Give me your attention .

sections.label: Section 1.1.32b
sections.translation: That ’ s not here .

sections.label: Section 1.1.32c
sections.translation: Do you summon it then .

sections.label: Section 1.1.33
sections.original: Immo ego tacebo , tu istinc ex cera cita ;
sections.translation: Well , I ’ ll be silent ; do you summon it from that wax there [*] ; for there my attention is at present , not in my breast .

sections.label: Section 1.1.35
sections.translation: I see your mistress , Calidorus .

sections.label: Section 1.1.35b
sections.translation: Where is she , prithee ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.36
sections.original: Eccam in tabellis porrectam : in cera cubat .
sections.translation: See , here she is at full length in the letter ; she ’ s lying upon the wax .

sections.label: Section 1.1.37
sections.translation: Now , may the Gods and Goddesses , inasmuch — [*]

sections.label: Section 1.1.37b
sections.translation: Preserve me from harm , to wit .

sections.label: Section 1.1.38
sections.translation: For a short season have I been like a summer plant [*] ; suddenly have I sprung up , suddenly have I withered .

sections.label: Section 1.1.40
sections.translation: Be silent , while I read the letter through .

sections.label: Section 1.1.40b
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you read it then ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.41
sections.translation: Phœnicium to her lover , Calidorus , by means of wax and string and letters , her exponents , sends health , and safety does she beg [*] of you , weeping , and with palpitating feelings , heart , and breast .

sections.label: Section 1.1.45
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ; I nowhere find , Pseudolus , this safety for me to send her back .

sections.label: Section 1.1.46b
sections.translation: What safety ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.46c
sections.translation: A silver one .

sections.label: Section 1.1.47
sections.translation: And do you wish to send her back a silver safety for one on wood [*] ? Consider what you ’ re about .

sections.label: Section 1.1.49
sections.original: Recita modo : ex tabellis iam faxo scies
sections.translation: Read on now ; I ’ ll soon cause you to know from the letter

sections.label: Section 1.1.50
sections.original: quam subito argento mi usus invento siet .
sections.translation: how suddenly there ’ s need for me for one of silver to be found .

sections.label: Section 1.1.51
sections.translation: The procurer has sold me , my love , for twenty minae , to a Macedonian officer from abroad . Before he departed hence , the Captain paid him fifteen minae ; only five minae now are remaining unpaid .

sections.label: Section 1.1.55
sections.original: ea caúsa miles hic reliquit symbolum ,
sections.translation: On that account the Captain left here a token — his own likeness impressed on wax by his ring — that he who should bring hither a token like to that , together with him the procurer might send me . The next day hence , on the Festival of Bacchus [*] , is the one fixed for this matter .

sections.label: Section 1.1.60
sections.translation: Well , that ’ s to-morrow ;

sections.label: Section 1.1.60b
sections.translation: my ruin is near at hand , unless I have some help in you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.61b
sections.translation: Let me read it through .

sections.label: Section 1.1.62
sections.original: Sino , nám mihi videor cúm ea fabularier ;
sections.translation: I permit you ; for I seem to myself to be talking to her . Read on ; the sweet and the hitter are you now mingling together for me .

sections.label: Section 1.1.64
sections.translation: Now our loves , our tenderness , our intimacy ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.65
sections.translation: our mirth , our dalliance , our talking , our sweet kisses , the close embrace of us lovers equally fond , the soft , dear kisses impressed on our tender lips , the delicious pressing of the swelling bosom ; of all these delights , I say , for me and for you as well ,

sections.label: Section 1.1.70
sections.translation: the severance , the destruction , and the downfal is at hand , unless there is some rescue for me in you or for you in me . I have taken care that you should know all these things that I have written ; now shall I make trial how far you love me , and how far you pretend to do so .

sections.label: Section 1.1.74
sections.translation: ’ Tis written , Pseudolus , in wretchedness .

sections.label: Section 1.1.74b
sections.translation: Alas ! very wretchedly [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.75
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you weep , then ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.75b
sections.translation: I ’ ve eyes of pumice stone [*] ; I can ’ t prevail upon them to squeeze out one tear even .

sections.label: Section 1.1.77
sections.translation: Why so ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.77b
sections.translation: My family was always a dryeyed one .

sections.label: Section 1.1.78
sections.translation: Won ’ t you attempt to assist me at all ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.78b
sections.translation: What shall I do for you ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.79
sections.translation: Alas !

sections.label: Section 1.1.79b
sections.original: Eheu ? id quidem hercle ne parsis : dabo .
sections.translation: Alas ! do you say ? Well , don ’ t be sparing of them , i ’ faith ; I ’ ll give you plenty .

sections.label: Section 1.1.80
sections.original: Miser sum , argentum nusquam invenio mutuom .
sections.translation: I ’ m distracted . I nowhere can find any money to borrow .
